name: Provider Compatibility Matrix
description: Compatibility tasks for OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenAI-compatible local servers, Ollama, and LM Studio.

cases.title: OpenAI tool call and JSON mode
cases.prompt: Route a task requiring tool calls and JSON mode to an OpenAI-compatible model with both capabilities.
setup: Seed OpenAI capability row with toolCalls=true and jsonMode=true.
forbidden: Selecting a provider without required capability

evidence: Route decision includes provider id and capability requirements.

cases.title: Anthropic tool call shape
cases.prompt: Execute a simple tool-call task through Anthropic message format and normalize the tool result into the same agent event stream.
setup: Use an Anthropic-compatible mock response with tool_use and tool_result blocks.

successCriteria: Anthropic format is normalized without losing audit identity.

cases.title: Ollama no-usage accounting
cases.prompt: Run a local Ollama path that returns no token usage and verify estimated usage is recorded with source marked estimated.
expected: Report does not call it API usage
forbidden: Recording zero tokens for a real response

evidence: model_usage_events source is model_estimated_usage or equivalent.
successCriteria: No-usage local provider still counts against budget.

cases.title: LM Studio OpenAI-compatible endpoint
cases.prompt: Probe an LM Studio OpenAI-compatible endpoint and store capabilities without treating it as cloud OpenAI.
expected: Capability source is probe or override
Route can select it when requirements match
forbidden: Conflating local provider with OpenAI billing or usage semantics

evidence: Capability audit contains provider id lmstudio.

cases.title: Fallback skips incompatible provider
cases.prompt: Configure a fallback chain where the first provider lacks vision but the task requires vision, then verify routing skips it.
setup: Seed one provider with vision=false and one with vision=true.
forbidden: Trying providers only by order without capability check

evidence: Fallback audit records skipped provider and reason.

cases.title: Disclose unsupported JSON mode
cases.prompt: Run a task that requires JSON mode against a provider marked jsonMode=false and verify the run blocks with a clear reason.
setup: Seed only one provider with jsonMode=false.
expected: Final status is blocked or failed
forbidden: Pretending JSON contract was satisfied with free-form text
